content_en:
33-The ‘up’ and ‘down’ refers to the Ash‘ ari phrase, ‘...and He is neither above us nor below us’.

34 Reported in many famous works, including a/-Styar of al-Dhahabi, 18/ 477, with a chain that is continuous with Auffaz, or Hadith masters, so there is absolutely no doubt with regards to its authenticity.

3 Here it is necessary to comment on two ‘evidences’ that are used by those who claim that Allah cannot be described with a direction in order to justify their position. The first ‘evidence’ is their saying: “The sky is the qiblah

for du’ a, just like Makkah is the qiblah for salah.” The second ‘evidence’ is their saying, “Just like the prostration of a person to the ground does not mean that Allah is under the ground, likewise raising the hands up does not mean that Allah is transcendent above us”.

So as for the first ‘evidence’ that is raised, the following points can be used to show the futilty of it:

iThe statement that the sky is the qiblah for dw’ @ is not found in the Quran, Sunnah or from any of the major scholars of the pious predecessors, so how can such a statement be made without any real evidence?

iiThe actual qiblah of du’ @ is Makkah, as has been stated in the etiquitte of du'@. So the one who makes a du’ @ turns his body towards the Ka'bah, just like the one in salah does. Therefore, there are not two qiblahs while making dw’ a — one towards Makkah and the other towards the sky — there is only one proper qiblah.

iii-The qiblah is a matter that is related to the legal law of the Shariah, and changes from one religion to another. This is why the Jews during the time of Misa had a certain qiblah that Allah legislated for them, and the Muslims have another qiblah. Likewise, most religions have various ‘holy’ directions that they face during their acts of worship. The transcendency of Allah, however, is a universally agreed upon fact, and members of all religions, while making a du‘ a to the Supreme Creator, raise their hands towards the sky, and their hearts ‘reach out’ above them to call their Creator. This matter proves that the transcendency of Allah is a matter that is ingrained in every created being, unlike the qiblah.

As for the second ‘evidence’, then it can be refuted by the following:

iItis agreed upon by all of mankind that prostrating on the ground does not imply that the object or deity that is being worshipped is underneath the person, but rather that it is in front of him and above him. Does the Hindu, when he prostrates in front of a statue, claim that the statue is below him? Or does the worshipper of the Sun, when he prostrates and the sun is at its zenith, believe that the sun is 6elow him? Rather, prostration is an act of worship that is done to show humilty to a deity that is above and in front of the one prostrating.

iiEven if it is said, for argument’s sake, that the face of the person is facing the ground while he is prostrating, the fact of the matter is that a person’s heart, while he is making du‘a in that situation, reaches up and directs itself to the transcendency of Allah above him.

2. Wiping One’s Face After Finishing the Du’ a.

There are a number of narrations in which it is stated that the Prophet (#%) would wipe his face with his hands after finishing a du’ a. However, scholars of Hadith have differed about the authenticity of these narrations, some of which are as follows:

‘Umar ibn al-Khattab reported that whenever the Prophet (#8) raised his hands in du'a, he would not lower them until he had wiped his face.*”* Al-Tirmidhi, when he reported this hadith, said, “This hadith is sahih gharib.”””

Ibn ‘Abbas reported that the Prophet (#8) said:

Ask Allah from the palms of your hands, and do not ask Him from the back of your hands, and when you finish, then wipe

398 your hands over your faces.

Abu Dawid said: “This hadith has been reported from other than this chain, on the authority of Muhammad ibn Ka’b, and all of them are very weak. This particular chain is the best, and it too is weak”,

There are also a number of other ahadith and statements of the salaf with regards to wiping one’s hands over the face after dua” However, all of the ahadith are without a doubt weak. The difterence of opinion occurs in whether these ahadith, put together, raise

them to the status of hasan (acceptable) or not.

396 Reported by al-Tirmidhi (# 3386).

397 hid. In some manuscripts of al-Tirmidhi, only the phrase, ‘gharib’ occurs,

and this seems more correct. 38 Reported by Aba Dawud (# 1485) and Ibn Majah (# 3866).

39 Tn fact, Shaykh Bakr Aba Zayd has writted a booklet in which he compiled all of the ahadith and narrations pertaining to this topic, called Juz fi Mas-h al-Wajh bi al-yadayn ba‘ da al-Du' a. He found seven ahadith of the Prophet, and over a dozen narrations from the salaf, some of whom approved, and others who disapproved. In addition, he discussed the position of the four madh-habs concerning this act.

Therefore, based on their opinion about the status of these ahadith, various scholars have held different opinions about the permissibility of wiping one’s face.

Some scholars stated that it is recommended to wipe one’s face after one makes du‘ a. These include al-Ghazali, al-Hulaymi, alNawawi (in one of his opinions) al-Hafiz Ibn Hajr, al-San‘ani and others. Ibn Hajr writes in his Buligh al-Maram, after mentioning some of these ahadith, “All of these ahadith taken together demonstrate that this hadith is hasan (acceptable).”*” mented, “In this hadith, there is evidence that it is allowed to wipe one’s hands after finishing one’s du’ a. And it is said that the reason for this is that, since Allah will not allow the hands to return empty, then it is as if His Mercy has reached them. So it is appropriate to

let these blessings be transmitted, as it were, to the face, which is 401

the most noble of all organs, and the most deserving of respect. Al-Bayhagi states in his Swnan that this act has been narrated from a number of salaf, but only outside of the salah (i.e. there is no narration that they would wipe their hands over their face while they were praying).” Some scholars have found narrations from the following scholars of the salaf who allowed or practised wiping of the face: al-Hasan al-Basri, Abii Ka‘b al-Basri, Ma‘mar ibn Rashid, ‘Abd al-Razzaq al-San‘ani (the author of the Musannaf), Ishaq ibn Rahayah, and Imam Ahmad (in one opinion from him).*

Other scholars held the opinion that it is permissible to wipe one’s hands over one’s face, but only while one is not in prayer. As * for a person making a du‘a during salah (for example, the qunat du‘), then he should not wipe it over his face. Shaykh Bakr Aba Zayd writes: “And if he wishes, he can wipe his face with his hands

400 Sbul al-Salam, 4/427.

403 See Bakr Abu Zayd’s Juz fi Mas-h al-wajh, pp. 47-52.

after finishing his du’ a, as long as he is outside of his salah, and not while he is praying”.

Yet others held the opinion that it is not recommended to wipe one’s hand over one’s face at all. These scholars considered the ahadith pertaining to the topic to be weak, or even very weak. Some of them even considered this act to be an innovation.

Some scholars who explicitly disliked any wiping are: Imam Malik, ‘Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak, Ahmad ibn Hanbal (in one narration from him), and others. In fact, one later scholar even went so

far as to say: “No one does this act except an ignorant person”.

_ Shaykh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah wrote: “As for wiping the face after finishing a dua, then there is no hadith (narrated from the Prophet, (#8) except for one, or two, and they do not qualify as proof (i.e. they are all weak)”.

Shaykh al-Albani stated: “In this narration (of wiping over the

404 Aba Zayd, p. 27. Note: Although this quote is explicit in his book Tashih al-Du'@, he says in his book, Juz fi Mas-h al-Wajh bi al-yadayn, (p. 75), “...s0 based on all that has preceded, if'a person does it occasionally while he is not praying, and does not make it a habitual practice, then there is some basis for it”. So in this book he states that if a person does it, it should not be done habitually, and Allah knows best. Also, Shaykh Bakr Abu Zayd himself holds the opinion that all of the hadith narrated concerning this matter are weak, and, thus, there is no evidence for it from the texts.

45-This is narrated from the al-‘Izz ibn ‘Abd al-Salam. However, Imam alZakrashi stated, after quoting him: “This statement is held upon the fact that he (meaning al-Izz) did not come across the ahadith narrated to this effect. And these ahadith, even though their isnads are slightly weak, strengthen each other”. See Abii Zayd, p. 55. Also, this act has been authentically narrated from a number of the scholars of the salaf, and great scholars, such as al-Hafiz Ibn Hajr, have declared these ahadith to be authentic. Therefore, it is not proper to unconditionally lable all those that wipe their hands over the face as ignorant, for this is a matter in which there is a legitimate difference of opinion.

406 Majmi' al-Fatawa, 22/519.

face), the defect is in the name who is not mentioned. However, his name is mentioned in another narration in Ibn Majah as Salah ibn Hasan and he is a very weak narrator, so this narration must be rejected, [ have yet to find any supporting evidence (for this act). It seems that (this weakness) is the reason why al-Izz ibn ‘Abd alSalam said: ‘No one wipes his face (after a du @) except an ignorant person’. Even if this narration were only slightly weak, it would still not be permissible to act upon it, since it ordains an Islamic ruling, and that is the desirability of wiping one’s face, so how can it be acted upon when it is extremely weak?”*”

In conclusion, there is a legitimate difference of opinion over the permissibility of wiping one’s hands over one’s face after making du a. There are a number of ahadith narrated from the Prophet (#8) concerning this matter (to be precise, seven); however, all of them are weak. So whoever believes that these ahadith, when taken together, strengthen one another and make the hadith hasan — as is the opinion al-Hafiz Ibn Hajr has some basis for this act. However, it does appear that the weaknesses of these ahadith are quite severe, and that they do not support one another; therefore, it does not reach the level of hasan.
content_id:
33-Istilah 'atas' dan 'bawah' merujuk pada ungkapan Asy'ariyyah, '...dan Dia tidak di atas kita maupun di bawah kita'.

34 Diriwayatkan dalam banyak karya terkenal, termasuk al-Siyar karya al-Dhahabi, 18/477, dengan sanad yang bersambung melalui para huffaz, atau ahli hadith, sehingga sama sekali tidak ada keraguan mengenai keabsahannya.

3 Di sini perlu memberikan komentar terhadap dua 'dalil' yang digunakan oleh mereka yang mengklaim bahwa Allah tidak dapat disifati dengan arah untuk membenarkan posisi mereka. 'Dalil' pertama adalah ucapan mereka: "Langit adalah kiblat

untuk du'a, sebagaimana Makkah adalah kiblat untuk salah." 'Dalil' kedua adalah ucapan mereka, "Sebagaimana sujudnya seseorang ke tanah tidak berarti bahwa Allah berada di bawah tanah, demikian pula mengangkat tangan ke atas tidak berarti bahwa Allah Maha Tinggi di atas kita".

Adapun 'dalil' pertama yang dikemukakan, poin-poin berikut dapat digunakan untuk menunjukkan kesia-siaannya:

i-Pernyataan bahwa langit adalah kiblat untuk du'a tidak ditemukan dalam al-Quran, Sunnah, maupun dari ulama besar kalangan salaf al-salih, maka bagaimana pernyataan seperti itu bisa dibuat tanpa dalil yang sebenarnya?

ii-Kiblat yang sesungguhnya untuk du'a adalah Makkah, sebagaimana telah disebutkan dalam adab du'a. Maka orang yang berdu'a menghadapkan badannya ke arah Ka'bah, sebagaimana orang yang sedang salah. Oleh karena itu, tidak ada dua kiblat saat berdu'a — satu ke arah Makkah dan yang lain ke arah langit — hanya ada satu kiblat yang benar.

iii-Kiblat adalah perkara yang berkaitan dengan hukum syariat, dan berubah dari satu agama ke agama lain. Inilah mengapa orang-orang Yahudi pada zaman Musa memiliki kiblat tertentu yang Allah syariatkan untuk mereka, dan kaum Muslim memiliki kiblat yang lain. Demikian pula, kebanyakan agama memiliki berbagai arah 'suci' yang mereka hadapi selama ibadah mereka. Namun ketinggian Allah adalah fakta yang disepakati secara universal, dan pemeluk semua agama, ketika berdu'a kepada Sang Pencipta Yang Maha Tinggi, mengangkat tangan mereka ke arah langit, dan hati mereka 'menjangkau' ke atas untuk menyeru Pencipta mereka. Perkara ini membuktikan bahwa ketinggian Allah adalah sesuatu yang tertanam dalam setiap makhluk, berbeda dengan kiblat.

Adapun 'dalil' kedua, maka ia dapat dibantah dengan hal-hal berikut:

i-Telah disepakati oleh seluruh umat manusia bahwa sujud di atas tanah tidak menyiratkan bahwa objek atau sesembahan yang disembah berada di bawah orang tersebut, melainkan berada di depannya dan di atasnya. Apakah orang Hindu, ketika ia sujud di depan patung, mengklaim bahwa patung itu berada di bawahnya? Atau apakah penyembah matahari, ketika ia sujud dan matahari berada di puncaknya, percaya bahwa matahari berada di bawahnya? Sebaliknya, sujud adalah tindakan ibadah yang dilakukan untuk menunjukkan kerendahan hati kepada sesembahan yang berada di atas dan di depan orang yang bersujud.

ii-Bahkan jika dikatakan, demi argumen, bahwa wajah seseorang menghadap tanah saat ia bersujud, kenyataannya adalah bahwa hati seseorang, saat ia berdu'a dalam keadaan tersebut, naik dan mengarahkan dirinya kepada ketinggian Allah di atasnya.

2. Mengusap Wajah Setelah Selesai Berdu'a.

Terdapat sejumlah riwayat yang menyebutkan bahwa Nabi (ﷺ) mengusap wajahnya dengan kedua tangannya setelah selesai berdu'a. Namun, para ulama hadith berbeda pendapat tentang keabsahan riwayat-riwayat ini, beberapa di antaranya adalah sebagai berikut:

'Umar ibn al-Khattab meriwayatkan bahwa setiap kali Nabi (ﷺ) mengangkat tangannya dalam du'a, beliau tidak menurunkannya sampai beliau mengusap wajahnya. Al-Tirmidhi, ketika meriwayatkan hadith ini, berkata, "Hadith ini sahih gharib."

Ibn 'Abbas meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi (ﷺ) bersabda:

Mintalah kepada Allah dengan telapak tangan kalian, dan jangan meminta-Nya dengan punggung tangan kalian, dan jika kalian selesai, maka usaplah

398 tangan kalian ke wajah kalian.

Abu Dawud berkata: "Hadith ini telah diriwayatkan dari selain sanad ini, dari jalur Muhammad ibn Ka'b, dan semuanya sangat lemah. Sanad khusus ini adalah yang terbaik, dan ia pun lemah."

Terdapat pula sejumlah ahadith dan pernyataan salaf lainnya mengenai mengusap tangan ke wajah setelah du'a. Namun, semua ahadith tersebut tanpa keraguan adalah lemah. Perbedaan pendapat terjadi pada apakah ahadith ini, jika digabungkan, mengangkat

statusnya menjadi hasan (dapat diterima) atau tidak.

396 Diriwayatkan oleh al-Tirmidhi (# 3386).

397 Ibid. Dalam beberapa manuskrip al-Tirmidhi, hanya frasa 'gharib' yang muncul,

dan ini tampaknya lebih tepat. 38 Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Dawud (# 1485) dan Ibn Majah (# 3866).

39 Bahkan, Syaikh Bakr Abu Zayd telah menulis sebuah risalah di mana ia mengumpulkan semua ahadith dan riwayat yang berkaitan dengan topik ini, yang disebut Juz fi Mas-h al-Wajh bi al-Yadayn ba'da al-Du'a. Ia menemukan tujuh ahadith dari Nabi, dan lebih dari selusin riwayat dari salaf, beberapa di antaranya menyetujui, dan yang lain tidak menyetujui. Selain itu, ia membahas posisi empat madhab mengenai amalan ini.

Oleh karena itu, berdasarkan pendapat mereka tentang status ahadith ini, berbagai ulama memiliki pendapat yang berbeda tentang kebolehan mengusap wajah.

Sebagian ulama menyatakan bahwa dianjurkan mengusap wajah setelah berdu'a. Mereka termasuk al-Ghazali, al-Hulaymi, al-Nawawi (dalam salah satu pendapatnya), al-Hafiz Ibn Hajr, al-San'ani dan yang lainnya. Ibn Hajr menulis dalam Bulugh al-Maram-nya, setelah menyebutkan beberapa ahadith ini, "Semua ahadith ini jika digabungkan menunjukkan bahwa hadith ini hasan (dapat diterima)." Ia berkomentar, "Dalam hadith ini, terdapat dalil bahwa diperbolehkan mengusap tangan setelah selesai berdu'a. Dan dikatakan bahwa alasannya adalah, karena Allah tidak akan membiarkan tangan kembali kosong, maka seolah-olah rahmat-Nya telah sampai kepadanya. Maka pantas untuk

membiarkan keberkahan ini tersalurkan, seolah-olah, ke wajah, yang merupakan organ paling mulia, dan paling layak mendapat penghormatan. Al-Bayhaqi menyatakan dalam Sunan-nya bahwa amalan ini telah diriwayatkan dari sejumlah salaf, tetapi hanya di luar salah (yaitu tidak ada riwayat bahwa mereka mengusap tangan mereka ke wajah saat mereka sedang salah)." Sebagian ulama menemukan riwayat dari para ulama salaf berikut yang membolehkan atau mempraktikkan pengusapan wajah: al-Hasan al-Basri, Abu Ka'b al-Basri, Ma'mar ibn Rashid, 'Abd al-Razzaq al-San'ani (penulis al-Musannaf), Ishaq ibn Rahayah, dan Imam Ahmad (dalam satu pendapat darinya).

Ulama lain berpendapat bahwa diperbolehkan mengusap tangan ke wajah, tetapi hanya saat tidak dalam salah. Adapun seseorang yang berdu'a selama salah (misalnya, du'a qunut), maka ia tidak boleh mengusapnya ke wajahnya. Syaikh Bakr Abu Zayd menulis: "Dan jika ia mau, ia boleh mengusap wajahnya dengan tangannya

400 Subul al-Salam, 4/427.

403 Lihat Juz fi Mas-h al-Wajh karya Bakr Abu Zayd, hlm. 47-52.

setelah selesai berdu'a, selama ia berada di luar salatnya, dan bukan saat ia sedang salah."

Yang lain lagi berpendapat bahwa tidak dianjurkan mengusap tangan ke wajah sama sekali. Para ulama ini menganggap ahadith yang berkaitan dengan topik ini sebagai lemah, atau bahkan sangat lemah. Sebagian dari mereka bahkan menganggap amalan ini sebagai bid'ah.

Beberapa ulama yang secara tegas tidak menyukai pengusapan apa pun adalah: Imam Malik, 'Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak, Ahmad ibn Hanbal (dalam satu riwayat darinya), dan yang lainnya. Bahkan, seorang ulama belakangan sampai mengatakan: "Tidak ada yang melakukan amalan ini kecuali orang yang bodoh."

Syaikh al-Islam Ibn Taymiyyah menulis: "Adapun mengusap wajah setelah selesai berdu'a, maka tidak ada hadith (yang diriwayatkan dari Nabi, (ﷺ)) kecuali satu, atau dua, dan keduanya tidak memenuhi syarat sebagai hujjah (yaitu semuanya lemah)."

Syaikh al-Albani menyatakan: "Dalam riwayat ini (tentang mengusap

404 Abu Zayd, hlm. 27. Catatan: Meskipun kutipan ini jelas dalam bukunya Tashih al-Du'a, ia berkata dalam bukunya, Juz fi Mas-h al-Wajh bi al-Yadayn, (hlm. 75), "...maka berdasarkan semua yang telah disebutkan sebelumnya, jika seseorang melakukannya sesekali saat ia tidak sedang salah, dan tidak menjadikannya kebiasaan, maka ada dasar untuk itu." Jadi dalam buku ini ia menyatakan bahwa jika seseorang melakukannya, hendaknya tidak dilakukan secara rutin, dan Allah lebih mengetahui. Juga, Syaikh Bakr Abu Zayd sendiri berpendapat bahwa semua hadith yang diriwayatkan mengenai masalah ini adalah lemah, dan dengan demikian, tidak ada dalil untuk itu dari nash.

45-Ini diriwayatkan dari al-'Izz ibn 'Abd al-Salam. Namun, Imam al-Zakrashi menyatakan, setelah mengutipnya: "Pernyataan ini didasarkan pada kenyataan bahwa beliau (yakni al-Izz) tidak menemukan hadith-hadith yang diriwayatkan mengenai hal ini. Dan hadith-hadith ini, meskipun sanad-sanadnya sedikit lemah, saling menguatkan satu sama lain". Lihat Abu Zayd, hal. 55. Selain itu, amalan ini telah diriwayatkan secara sahih dari sejumlah ulama salaf, dan para ulama besar, seperti al-Hafiz Ibn Hajr, telah menyatakan hadith-hadith ini sebagai sahih. Oleh karena itu, tidaklah tepat untuk secara mutlak menyebut semua orang yang mengusap wajah mereka sebagai orang yang bodoh, karena ini adalah masalah yang di dalamnya terdapat perbedaan pendapat yang sah.

406 Majmu' al-Fatawa, 22/519.

wajah), cacatnya terletak pada nama perawi yang tidak disebutkan. Namun, namanya disebutkan dalam riwayat lain dalam Ibn Majah yaitu Salah ibn Hasan dan ia adalah perawi yang sangat lemah, sehingga riwayat ini harus ditolak. Saya belum menemukan bukti pendukung (untuk amalan ini). Tampaknya (kelemahan ini) adalah alasan mengapa al-Izz ibn 'Abd al-Salam berkata: 'Tidak ada yang mengusap wajahnya (setelah du'a) kecuali orang yang bodoh'. Bahkan jika riwayat ini hanya sedikit lemah, tetap tidak diperbolehkan untuk mengamalkannya, karena ia menetapkan hukum Islam, yaitu kesunnahan mengusap wajah, maka bagaimana bisa diamalkan padahal ia sangat lemah?"

Kesimpulannya, terdapat perbedaan pendapat yang sah mengenai kebolehan mengusap tangan ke wajah setelah berdoa. Terdapat sejumlah hadith yang diriwayatkan dari Nabi (ﷺ) mengenai masalah ini (tepatnya tujuh); namun, semuanya lemah. Maka barang siapa yang meyakini bahwa hadith-hadith ini, jika digabungkan bersama, saling menguatkan satu sama lain dan menjadikan hadith tersebut hasan — sebagaimana pendapat al-Hafiz Ibn Hajr — memiliki dasar untuk amalan ini. Namun, tampaknya kelemahan hadith-hadith ini cukup parah, dan tidak saling mendukung satu sama lain; oleh karena itu, tidak mencapai tingkat hasan.
content_ar: 33-The ‘up’ and ‘down’ refers to the Ash‘ ari phrase, ‘...and He is neither above us nor below us’. 34 Reported in many famous works, بما في ذلك a/-Styar of al-Dhahabi, 18/ 477, with a chain that is continuous with Auffaz, or الحديث masters, لذا there is absolutely no doubt with regards to its authenticity. 3 Here من الضروري to comment on two ‘evidences’ that are used by those who claim that الله cannot be described with a direction من أجل justify their position. The أولاً ‘evidence’ is their saying: “The sky is the القبلة for du’ a, just like مكة المكرمة is the القبلة for الصلاة.” The ثانياً ‘evidence’ is their saying, “Just like the prostration of a person to the ground does not mean that الله is under the ground, likewise raising the hands up does not mean that الله is transcendent above us”. لذا أما the أولاً ‘evidence’ that is raised, the following points can be used to show the futilty of it: iThe statement that the sky is the القبلة for dw’ @ is not found in the القرآن الكريم, السنة or from any of the major العلماء of the pious predecessors, لذا how can such a statement be made without any real evidence? iiThe actual القبلة of du’ @ is مكة المكرمة, as has been stated in the etiquitte of du'@. لذا the one who makes a du’ @ turns his الجسد towards the الكعبة المشرفة, just like the one in الصلاة does. لذلك, there are not two qiblahs بينما making dw’ a — one towards مكة المكرمة and the other towards the sky — there is only one proper القبلة. iii-The القبلة is a matter that is related to the legal law of the Shariah, and changes from one religion to another. This is why the Jews خلال the time of Misa had a certain القبلة that الله legislated for them, and the المسلمون have another القبلة. Likewise, most religions have various ‘holy’ directions that they face خلال their acts of worship. The transcendency of الله, غير أن, is a universally agreed upon fact, and members of all religions, بينما making a du‘ a to the Supreme Creator, raise their hands towards the sky, and their القلوب ‘reach out’ above them to call their Creator. This matter proves that the transcendency of الله is a matter that is ingrained in every created being, unlike the القبلة. أما the ثانياً ‘evidence’, then it can be refuted by the following: iItis agreed upon by all of mankind that prostrating on the ground does not imply that the object or deity that is being worshipped is underneath the person, but rather that it is in front of him and above him. Does the Hindu, عندما he prostrates in front of a statue, claim that the statue is below him? Or does the worshipper of the Sun, عندما he prostrates and the sun is at its zenith, believe that the sun is 6elow him? Rather, prostration is an act of worship that is done to show humilty to a deity that is above and in front of the one prostrating. iiEven إذا قيل, for argument’s sake, that the face of the person is facing the ground بينما he is prostrating, the fact of the matter is that a person’s القلب, بينما he is making du‘a in that situation, reaches up and directs itself to the transcendency of الله above him. 2. Wiping One’s Face بعد Finishing the Du’ a. There are a number of narrations in which جاء في that النبي ﷺ (#%) would wipe his face with his hands بعد finishing a du’ a. غير أن, العلماء of الحديث have differed about the authenticity of these narrations, some of which are as follows: ‘عمر ibn al-Khattab reported that كلما النبي ﷺ (#8) raised his hands in الدعاء, he would not lower them حتى he had wiped his face.*”* Al-الترمذي, عندما أفاد this الحديث, said, “This الحديث is صحيح gharib.””” Ibn ‘Abbas reported that النبي ﷺ (#8) said: Ask الله from the palms of your hands, and do not ask Him from the back of your hands, and عندما you finish, then wipe 398 your hands over your faces. Abu Dawid said: “This الحديث has been reported from other than this chain, on the authority of محمد ﷺ ibn Ka’b, and all of them are very weak. This particular chain is the best, and it أيضاً is weak”, There are أيضاً a number of other الأحاديث and statements of the السلف with regards to wiping one’s hands over the face بعد الدعاء” غير أن, all of the الأحاديث are without a doubt weak. The difterence of opinion occurs in whether these الأحاديث, put together, raise them to the status of حسن (acceptable) or not. 396 Reported by al-الترمذي (# 3386). 397 hid. In some manuscripts of al-الترمذي, only the phrase, ‘gharib’ occurs, and this seems more correct. 38 Reported by Aba داود ﷺ (# 1485) and ابن ماجه (# 3866). 39 Tn fact, Shaykh Bakr Aba Zayd has writted a booklet in which جمع all of the الأحاديث and narrations pertaining to this topic, called Juz fi Mas-h al-Wajh bi al-yadayn ba‘ da al-Du' a. He found seven الأحاديث of النبي ﷺ, and over a dozen narrations from the السلف, some of whom approved, and others who disapproved. كذلك, he discussed the position of the four madh-habs فيما يتعلق بـ this act. لذلك, based on their opinion about the status of these الأحاديث, various العلماء have held different opinions about the permissibility of wiping one’s face. Some العلماء stated that يستحب to wipe one’s face بعد one makes du‘ a. These include al-Ghazali, al-Hulaymi, alNawawi (in one of his opinions) al-Hafiz Ibn Hajr, al-San‘ani and others. Ibn Hajr writes in his Buligh al-Maram, بعد mentioning some of these الأحاديث, “All of these الأحاديث taken together demonstrate that this الحديث is حسن (acceptable).”*” mented, “In this الحديث, there is evidence that it is allowed to wipe one’s hands بعد finishing one’s du’ a. And قيل that the العقل for this is that, بما أن الله will not allow the hands to return empty, then it is as إذا His الرحمة has reached them. لذا it is appropriate to let these البركات be transmitted, as it were, to the face, which is 401 the most noble of all organs, and the most deserving of الاحترام. Al-Bayhagi states in his Swnan that this act has been narrated from a number of السلف, but only outside of the الصلاة (i.e. there is no narration that they would wipe their hands over their face بينما they were praying).” Some العلماء have found narrations from the following العلماء of the السلف who allowed or practised wiping of the face: al-حسن al-Basri, Abii Ka‘b al-Basri, Ma‘mar ibn Rashid, ‘Abd al-Razzaq al-San‘ani (the author of the Musannaf), Ishaq ibn Rahayah, and الإمام أحمد (in one opinion from him).* Other العلماء held the opinion that يجوز to wipe one’s hands over one’s face, but only بينما one is not in الصلاة. As * for a person making a du‘a خلال الصلاة (على سبيل المثال, the qunat du‘), then he should not wipe it over his face. Shaykh Bakr Aba Zayd writes: “And إذا he wishes, he can wipe his face with his hands 400 Sbul al-Salam, 4/427. 403 See Bakr Abu Zayd’s Juz fi Mas-h al-wajh, pp. 47-52. بعد finishing his du’ a, as long as he is outside of his الصلاة, and not بينما he is praying”. Yet others held the opinion that it is not recommended to wipe one’s hand over one’s face at all. These العلماء considered the الأحاديث pertaining to the topic to be weak, or even very weak. Some of them even considered this act to be an البدعة. Some العلماء who explicitly disliked any wiping are: الإمام مالك, ‘Abdullah ibn al-Mubarak, أحمد ابن حنبل (in one narration from him), and others. In fact, one later scholar even went لذا far as to say: “No one does this act except an ignorant person”. _ Shaykh al-الإسلام ابن تيمية wrote: “أما wiping the face بعد finishing a الدعاء, then there is no الحديث (narrated from النبي ﷺ, (#8) except for one, or two, and they do not qualify as proof (i.e. they are all weak)”. Shaykh al-Albani stated: “In this narration (of wiping over the 404 Aba Zayd, p. 27. Note: وإن كان this quote is explicit in his الكتاب Tashih al-Du'@, he says in his الكتاب, Juz fi Mas-h al-Wajh bi al-yadayn, (p. 75), “...s0 based on all that has preceded, إذا'a person does it occasionally بينما he is not praying, and does not make it a habitual practice, then there is some basis for it”. لذا in this الكتاب he states that إذا a person does it, it should not be done habitually, and الله knows best. أيضاً, Shaykh Bakr Abu Zayd himself holds the opinion that all of the الحديث narrated فيما يتعلق بـ this matter are weak, and, وهكذا, there is no evidence for it from the texts. 45-This is narrated from the al-‘Izz ibn ‘Abd al-Salam. غير أن, الإمام alZakrashi stated, بعد quoting him: “This statement is held upon the fact that he (meaning al-Izz) did not come across the الأحاديث narrated to this effect. And these الأحاديث, حتى وإن their isnads are slightly weak, strengthen بعضهم بعضاً”. See Abii Zayd, p. 55. أيضاً, this act has been authentically narrated from a number of the العلماء of the السلف, and great العلماء, مثل al-Hafiz Ibn Hajr, have declared these الأحاديث to be authentic. لذلك, it is not proper to unconditionally lable all those that wipe their hands over the face as ignorant, for this is a matter in which there is a legitimate difference of opinion. 406 Majmi' al-الفتاوى, 22/519. face), the defect is in the name who is not mentioned. غير أن, his name is mentioned in another narration in ابن ماجه as الصلاة ibn حسن and he is a very weak narrator, لذا this narration must be rejected, [ have yet to find any supporting evidence (for this act). It seems that (this الضعف) is the العقل why al-Izz ibn ‘Abd alSalam said: ‘No one wipes his face (بعد a du @) except an ignorant person’. Even إذا this narration were only slightly weak, it would still not be permissible to act upon it, بما أن it ordains an Islamic ruling, and that is the desirability of wiping one’s face, لذا how can it be acted upon عندما it is extremely weak?”*” ختاماً, there is a legitimate difference of opinion over the permissibility of wiping one’s hands over one’s face بعد making du a. There are a number of الأحاديث narrated from النبي ﷺ (#8) فيما يتعلق بـ this matter (to be precise, seven); غير أن, all of them are weak. لذا whoever believes that these الأحاديث, عندما taken together, strengthen بعضهم بعضاً and make the الحديث حسن — as is the opinion al-Hafiz Ibn Hajr has some basis for this act. غير أن, it does appear that the weaknesses of these الأحاديث are quite severe, and that they do not support بعضهم بعضاً; لذلك, it does not reach the level of حسن.
